display.purpose: Stat / metric values only — the loudest type in the system.

title.purpose: Section headings, card and panel titles.

body.purpose: Default reading size for prose and controls.

label.purpose: Uppercase functional micro-labels: nav sections, stat keys, event kickers.

mono.purpose: Anything quoted verbatim from the machine: diffs, IDs, configs, agent output.

shadows.name: overlay
shadows.purpose: The only shadow. Reserved for the floating modal dialog to lift it off the dimmed backdrop.

motion.name: state
motion.purpose: Default ease-out transition for button, nav, tab, and card hover state changes. Collapses to instant under prefers-reduced-motion.

motion.name: topbar-blur
motion.purpose: Backdrop blur on the sticky translucent top bar only.

motion.name: shimmer
motion.purpose: Skeleton loading placeholder shimmer. Static under prefers-reduced-motion.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The active/primary action — approve, submit, start loop.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 7px; padding: 8px 14px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm, 7px); background: var(--accent, #6ea8fe); color: #0b0f17; border: 1px solid transparent; font-weight: 550; cursor: pointer; transition: 0.12s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #85b6ff; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--text, #e6ebf2); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.45; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Approve (Good) Button
components.description: Gate approval — approve the plan or the diff.
components.css: .ds-btn-good { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 7px; padding: 8px 14px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm, 7px); background: var(--good, #4ec9a3); color: #06231a; border: 1px solid transparent; font-weight: 550; cursor: pointer; transition: 0.12s; } .ds-btn-good:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--text, #e6ebf2); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Destructive (Bad) Button
components.description: Destructive / reject action — request changes, cancel.
components.css: .ds-btn-bad { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 7px; padding: 8px 14px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm, 7px); background: transparent; color: var(--bad, #e0685f); border: 1px solid #4a2a2a; font-weight: 550; cursor: pointer; transition: 0.12s; } .ds-btn-bad:hover { background: rgba(224, 104, 95, 0.12); } .ds-btn-bad:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--bad, #e0685f); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Status Pill
components.description: Lifecycle-state badge. Amber = awaiting operator; blue = machine working; green = shipped; coral = failed.
components.css: .ds-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 3px 9px; border-radius: 20px; font-size: 11.5px; font-weight: 600; border: 1px solid transparent; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-pill .ds-dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 50%; background: currentColor; } .ds-pill-review { color: var(--warn, #e0b657); background: rgba(224, 182, 87, 0.13); } .ds-pill-working { color: var(--accent, #6ea8fe); background: rgba(110, 168, 254, 0.12); } .ds-pill-done { color: var(--good, #4ec9a3); background: rgba(78, 201, 163, 0.13); }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Recedes below its card — a dark Ink well; border shifts to Signal Blue on focus.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; padding: 9px 12px; background: var(--bg, #0d1017); color: var(--text, #e6ebf2); border: 1px solid var(--border, #2a3344); border-radius: var(--radius-sm, 7px); outline: none; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--text-dim, #9aa7bd); } .ds-input:focus { border-color: var(--accent, #6ea8fe); }

components.name: Task Card
components.description: Board card. Flat at rest; hover brightens the border and lifts 1px. Attention variant (task at a gate) takes a full amber border + faint amber tint — never a side-stripe.
components.html:
Add retry to evidence agent
planning · 4m ago
components.css: .ds-task-card { background: var(--bg-1, #141922); border: 1px solid var(--border-soft, #212938); border-radius: var(--radius-sm, 7px); padding: 12px 13px; cursor: pointer; transition: 0.12s; } .ds-task-card:hover { border-color: var(--border, #2a3344); transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-task-title { font-weight: 600; margin-bottom: 7px; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: var(--text, #e6ebf2); } .ds-task-meta { display: flex; gap: 8px; font-size: 11.5px; color: var(--text-faint, #808ca4); }

components.name: Nav Item
components.description: Sidebar navigation row. Muted at rest, fills a lighter navy on hover/active.
components.css: .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; padding: 8px 10px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm, 7px); color: var(--text-dim, #9aa7bd); cursor: pointer; font-weight: 500; transition: 0.12s; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: var(--bg-2, #1b2230); color: var(--text, #e6ebf2); } .ds-nav-item.ds-active { background: var(--bg-3, #232c3d); color: var(--text, #e6ebf2); }

components.name: Gate Panel
components.description: The signature component. An amber-bordered block that hosts a plan/diff approval — the one place the operator must act.
components.html:
Approve the diff
Evidence passed. Checker approved. 3 files changed.
components.css: .ds-gate { border: 1px solid var(--warn, #e0b657); background: rgba(224, 182, 87, 0.07); border-radius: var(--radius, 10px); padding: 16px; } .ds-gate-title { color: var(--warn, #e0b657); font-size: 15px; font-weight: 650; margin-bottom: 8px; } .ds-gate-body { color: var(--text-dim, #9aa7bd); font-size: 13px; }

narrative.overview: Deputy is the console a single operator sits at while a crew of agents does the building. It should feel like a well-run, dimly-lit control room: quiet at rest, dense with live state, and instantly legible under load. Depth comes from stacking four tonal shades of a near-black navy, not from drop shadows. It foregrounds the machinery honestly — diffs as real colored patches, the agent stream as live tool calls and thinking, evidence and checker verdicts in plain view — and never abstracts the work into a cheerful summary.
keyCharacteristics: Deep navy tonal field (#0d1017 → #232c3d), depth by layering not shadow
Color is semantic and rationed — amber = your turn, blue = the machine working
Dense, keyboard-first, glanceable; no onboarding scaffolding
Inter for UI, a monospace stack for anything machine-truthful (diffs, IDs, logs, configs)
Composed under motion: feedback informs, it never performs busyness

rules.name: The Amber-Is-Yours Rule
rules.body: Gate Amber (#e0b657) is reserved exclusively for states that await the operator — plan review, human review, the gate panel, attention strips. The machine's own working states are always blue. Never spend amber on decoration; it dilutes the one color that means act.

rules.name: The Rationed-Accent Rule
rules.body: Signal Blue marks the primary path and machine activity, nothing else. If a screen has more than a few blue elements, something non-primary has been miscolored.

rules.name: The Tonal-Depth Rule
rules.body: Elevation is expressed by climbing the navy ramp, never by adding shadow to inline surfaces. Shadow means 'this floats above the page,' and only the modal earns that.

rules.name: The Machine-Speaks-Mono Rule
rules.body: Anything reproduced literally from the system — a diff, an ID, a file path, a config blob, an agent's raw output — is set in the mono stack. Prose about the work is set in Inter. The font boundary tells the operator what is quoted versus what is narrated.

dos: Do reserve Gate Amber (#e0b657) exclusively for states that need the operator.
Do express elevation by climbing the navy ramp and hairline borders; keep inline surfaces shadow-free.
Do set anything quoted verbatim from the machine in the mono stack; narrate about the work in Inter.
Do keep Signal Blue rationed to the primary path and machine-working states.
Do pair every state color with its text label and dot; never rely on hue alone.
Do hold WCAG AA (4.5:1 body); step muted text up to Muted Steel for anything readable.
donts: Don't ship generic AI-SaaS: no cream hero, no purple-gradient-everything, no hero-metric template, no identical card grids, no tracked uppercase eyebrow above every section.
Don't turn the agent stream into a consumer chatbot — no chat bubbles, mascots, rounded-everything, or emoji-forward tone. It is telemetry.
Don't drift into enterprise-Jira clutter: gray-on-gray, deeply nested panels, undifferentiated density.
Don't cosplay as a terminal: no matrix-green glow, scanlines, or neon cyberpunk aesthetic.
Don't use a colored border-left/border-right stripe anywhere — there are none in this system. Signal 'needs you' with a full amber border + tint, never a side edge.
Don't add drop shadows to inline cards or resting chrome; the modal overlay is the only element that earns a shadow.
Don't introduce a second general-purpose accent; Iris has assigned jobs and Signal Blue owns the primary path.
